html:
“Can be planted in ground”

-

The Spiky Bush is an in-game flora commonly found in Marsh and Desert biomes. It acts as a source of Twigs, like Saplings. Spiky Bushes are not affected by Winter cold and will regrow after 4 game days during both Summer and in Winter. Harvesting Twigs from a spiky bush damages by 3 points.

Spiky Bushes can be dug up with a Shovel and replanted in a new location. This gives a Twig and an equivalent Sapling and is the safer way of getting Twigs, as digging and picking up a Spiky Bush does not damage the player. The bushes themselves can also be used as an effective fuel for Campfires, though they are not renewable. In this regard, they are similar to Saplings and Grass Tufts.


Spiky Bushes can't be planted on Checkerboard Floorings, Wooden Floorings, Cobblestones, Carpeted Floorings, Rocky Turfs, Sandy Turfs, and Cave Rock Turfs, unless it has been planted before these turfs were placed.

Spiky Bushes can also be found in the Desert biome.

Tips

  • Spiky Bushes can be safely harvested with a Bramble Husk equipped, without losing health. The Bramble Husk also won't lose durability.
  • With the Don't Starve Mega Update new mechanic changes, players can now collect, pick up items, craft and load weapons while mounted on beefalos. This makes harvesting twigs from spiky bushes have no player penalty at all, while the thorns damage goes to the beefalo, increasing its ornery tendency points.

Trivia

  • There are two texture sets in the game, one in darker gray the other in very light, ashen gray. This is merely a cosmetic, visual difference and has no impact on the game.

Gallery

  • A burnt Spiky Bush.

“It looks thorny.”
–Wilson
“It looks sharp.”
–Willow
“Is thorny.”
–Wolfgang
“A bush that always hurts.”
–Wendy
“BRAMBLES.”
–WX-78
“A cluster of brambles.”
–Wickerbottom
“That's a bramble.”
–Woodie
“It's twisted and thorny, just like me!”
–Maxwell
“An abundant source of twigs.”
–Wagstaff
“A shrub ön guard.”
–Wigfrid
“Hope we don't fall on that.”
–Webber
“I don't need spikes.”
–Walani
“A prickly customer.”
–Warly
“She's got wee swords.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS BUSH O' THE SPIKY BITS”
–Wilba
“Twisty friend”
–Wormwood
“I'm beginning to think it's trying to keep people away.”
–Wheeler
“Gnarly little bush.”
–Winona
“Yikes! Spikes.”
–Wortox
“Lots of these in swamp, flort.”
–Wurt
“It might be a bog monster in disguise.”
–Walter
“Calling it a bush is quite charitable.”
–Wanda
“Can be planted in ground”
-

The bushes themselves can also be used as an effective fuel for Campfires, though they are not renewable. In this regard, they are similar to Saplings and Grass Tufts.
Spiky Bushes can't be planted on Checkerboard Floorings, Wooden Floorings, Cobblestones, Carpeted Floorings, Rocky Turfs, Sandy Turfs, and Cave Rock Turfs, unless it has been planted before these turfs were placed.
Spiky Bushes can also be found in the Desert biome.

It acts as a source of [[Twigs]], like [[Saplings]]. Spiky Bushes are not affected by [[Winter]] cold and will regrow after 4 game days during both [[Summer]] and in Winter. Harvesting Twigs from a spiky bush damages {{hp}} by 3 points. Spiky Bushes can be dug up with a [[Shovel]] and replanted in a new location. This gives a Twig and an equivalent Sapling and is the safer way of getting Twigs, as digging and picking up a Spiky Bush does not damage the player. The bushes themselves can also be used as an effective fuel for [[Campfires]], though they are not renewable. In this regard, they are similar to Saplings and [[Grass Tuft]]s. __NOTOC__ Spiky Bushes can't be planted on [[Checkerboard Flooring]]s, [[Wooden Flooring]]s, [[Cobblestone]]s, [[Carpeted Flooring]]s, [[Rocky Turf]]s, [[Sandy Turf]]s, and [[Cave Rock Turf]]s, unless it has been planted before these [[turf]]s were placed. Spiky Bushes can also be found in the [[Desert]] biome. == {{pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Spiky Bushes can be safely harvested with a [[Bramble Husk]] equipped, without losing health. The Bramble Husk also won't lose durability. * With the [[Don't Starve Mega Update]] new mechanic changes, players can now collect, pick up items, craft and load weapons while mounted on beefalos. This makes harvesting twigs from spiky bushes have no player {{Hp}} penalty at all, while the thorns damage goes to the beefalo, increasing its ornery tendency points. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * There are two texture sets in the game, one in darker gray the other in very light, ashen gray.
